design_philosophy.core_loop: Race against time and a computer-controlled opponent across the continental US, managing speed via turbo boost while dealing with engine heat and police interference. Periodically choose vehicle upgrades at checkpoints.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. resource management (turbo heat) vs. competitive pressure from AI opponent. Stakes escalate when girlfriend transfers to opponent's car if player falls behind.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-speed cross-country racing in a Ferrari F40, competing directly against an AI rival for dominance and romantic stakes.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Linear single-path design (no branching roads unlike original Out Run) creates focused, arcade-paced progression. The 'girlfriend as prize' mechanic and AI rival create narrative urgency beyond pure time trials. Turbo boost system with thermal management adds resource tension. Mandatory upgrades at checkpoints force strategic decisions rather than open customization.

difficulty.continues_system: Arcade continues available; home versions vary by platform
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoints placed after approximately four cities. Player must reach checkpoint before opponent to retain competitive standing. Falling behind transfers girlfriend to opponent's car, adding narrative consequence.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player chooses between automatic and manual transmission. At each checkpoint, player selects one of three upgrades (Hi-Power Engine, Special Turbo, Super Grip Tires), allowing some strategic adaptation to current conditions.

progression.upgrade_system: Mandatory checkpoint-based upgrade selection: Hi-Power Engine, Special Turbo, or Super Grip Tires. Three choices available at each sub-goal. Upgrades carry across the entire race.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Single fixed path from New York City to Los Angeles. Progression is time-gated and opponent-gated (must reach checkpoints before CPU rival).
replayability_hooks: Compete for 1,000,000 point bonus by beating opponent while girlfriend is in player's car
Challenge of retrieving girlfriend if lost to opponent
Different upgrade strategies and timing decisions per playthrough

world_building_techniques: Geographic progression from NYC to LA creates implicit American road-trip narrative
Girlfriend presence in car establishes romantic subplot
CPU opponent with visible vehicle (silver Porsche 959) creates personal rivalry
Police interference adds environmental storytelling (law enforcement as obstacles)
Ending scene plays upon final checkpoint reaching, providing closure

faction_indicators: Player vehicle (Ferrari F40) vs. opponent vehicle (Porsche 959) - status/performance competition
Girlfriend as transferable prize creates romantic/territorial stakes
danger_signals: Police cars that attempt to stop player, can be rammed off-road
Engine overheat mechanic punishes excessive turbo use
Opponent reaching checkpoints first triggers girlfriend loss
environmental_storytelling: Progression through ~16 cities across continental US (4 per checkpoint section)
Side-of-road obstacles that police can be rammed into, suggesting hazardous terrain
Turbo boost as arcade-style mechanical system distinct from realistic driving
